evals.description: Trigger test: activates on text preprocessing question
evals.prompt: I'm ingesting user reviews from a web scraper and they're full of HTML tags, emoji, and inconsistent casing. What should my text cleaning pipeline look like before I train a classifier?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on embedding generation question
evals.prompt: I want to generate embeddings for 500,000 product descriptions and store them for semantic search. What model and approach should I use?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on semantic search question
evals.prompt: How do I build a semantic search engine over a corpus of 100k support articles so that users can find relevant articles by natural language query, not just keyword match?

evals.description: Core task: text preprocessing pipeline code
evals.prompt: Write a Python function that takes raw HTML text, strips markup, normalizes unicode, lowercases, removes URLs and emails, and collapses whitespace.

evals.description: Core task: semantic search with FAISS
evals.prompt: Show me how to build a local semantic search index over a list of strings using FAISS and sentence-transformers, then query it.
I have a dataset of customer support tickets labeled with categories (billing, technical, general). How do I fine-tune a small pretrained model for this classification task using HuggingFace?
I need to extract company names, locations, and person names from news articles. Show me two approaches - one fast and one more accurate.

evals.description: Core task: document chunking for RAG
evals.prompt: I'm building a RAG system and need to chunk long PDF documents before embedding them. What's the best chunking strategy and how do I implement it?
I want to automatically generate a 2-3 sentence summary of each article in my news pipeline. Should I use extractive or abstractive summarization and how do I implement it?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about chunking without overlap
evals.prompt: I'm chunking my documents into 512-character blocks with no overlap and my RAG system gives poor answers when the relevant information spans a chunk boundary. What's wrong?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about embedding HTML without preprocessing
evals.prompt: My semantic search returns terrible results even though I'm using a good embedding model. I'm embedding the raw HTML from our CMS directly. Could that be the issue?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: recommends small model first, not GPT-4 for all tasks
evals.prompt: I need to classify customer emails into 5 categories. Should I just call GPT-4 for every email?
